Title: Comparative genomic analyses reveal the genetic basis of the yellow-seed trait in Brassica napus

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in flavonoid/proanthocyanidin transport or sequestration required for pigment accumulation in the seed coat rather than a core catalytic biosynthetic conversion
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing seed coats; higher in black-seeded lines than yellow-seeded lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat pigmentation and associated yellow/black seed color trait
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced BnTT18 expression is associated with decreased proanthocyanidin accumulation and yellow seed coat; higher expression is associated with black seed coat pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis in developing seed coats; promoter binding assay by yeast one-hybrid with BnA09MYB47a; sequence comparison among genetic backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (DEG analysis in RNAi-BnA09myb47a lines); DAP-seq for BnA09MYB47a binding
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target/marker for selecting yellow-seed trait through reduced proanthocyanidin deposition, though likely best deployed via upstream regulator manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnTT18 is a key downstream flavonoid-related gene associated with seed coat pigmentation in B. napus. 2. Its expression is higher in black-seeded backgrounds and reduced in yellow-seeded materials. 3. BnA09MYB47a directly binds the BnTT18 promoter, linking the causal regulator to proanthocyanidin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although categorized here as a pathway-related gene because it directly affects metabolite accumulation, TT18 classically acts in transport/sequestration rather than enzymatic synthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: ZY821 allele; GH06 allele; no2127; quintaA; shengli3; ZS11 compared
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression and RNAi transgenic constructs used; specific promoter NA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Developing seed coat-associated context implied; exact pattern NA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid/proanthocyanidin biosynthesis and accumulation in seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/positively regulates BnTT18 expression by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator underlying the yellow-seed versus black-seed trait through control of proanthocyanidin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat pigmentation determining yellow versus black seed color; relevant to desirable yellow-seed trait in rapeseed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of the ZY821 allele in yellow-seeded GH06 darkens seeds and increases flavonoid/proanthocyanidin metabolites; RNAi silencing decreases these metabolites and promotes yellow seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping/comparative genomics; sequence variation analysis; transgenic overexpression; RNAi knockdown; metabolite profiling by UPLC-HESI-MS/MS; RNA-seq differential expression; subcellular localization; yeast one-hybrid; DAP-seq
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative genomics; QTL-seq; RNA-seq; metabolomics by UPLC-HESI-MS/MS; DAP-seq
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding yellow-seeded rapeseed by allele selection, promoter weakening, or genome editing to reduce BnA09MYB47a activity; can be combined with favorable oil/protein backgrounds while monitoring seed coat-associated effects
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BnA09MYB47a was identified within the major seed coat color QTL and differs between yellow- and black-seeded B. napus lines. 2. Overexpression of the black-seed allele increases seed coat pigmentation, while RNAi reduces pigmentation and proanthocyanidin-related metabolites. 3. BnA09MYB47a localizes to the nucleus and directly binds the BnTT18 promoter, thereby promoting flavonoid/proanthocyanidin accumulation in developing seed coats.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core causal gene highlighted by the supplementary data. The study centers on seed coat color, but the nutrition-relevant metabolites specifically supported are flavonoid/proanthocyanidin compounds in the seed coat.
